brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Add the third table — era_records. Each engagement has one. The detail column is what someone wrote down at the time, in their handwriting, with a quill in some cases. Join on engagement_id. Use 'r' for the alias. Keep the anomaly filter.

expected.sql: SELECT e.era, e.year, c.name, r.detail, r.location FROM chrono_engagements e INNER JOIN chrono_clients c ON e.client_id = c.client_id INNER JOIN era_records r ON r.engagement_id = e.engagement_id WHERE e.anomaly_note IS NOT NULL

hints.text: Carol: 'Second join takes era_records — alias it r. Match r.engagement_id to e.engagement_id.'
Carol: 'INNER JOIN era_records r ON r.engagement_id = e.engagement_id. Same shape as the first join.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: Carol slides her finger across the new columns. 'Saqqara. Mala Strana. The Hemlock Room. Census three thousand.' She pauses on a fifth detail. 'Internal.' She doesn't comment further. 'Pull just his rows next, oldest first.'
